title: Deliver exact 3-language boilerplate parity and archive retirement
description:
# Objective
Execute the full AICD migration from archive-backed audit state to shipped multi-language boilerplate parity.

# Scope
This epic owns every requirement in `PLAN.md`, every tracked archived source file in `files.txt`, and the full migration obligation across Python, TypeScript, and Rust.

# Required outcome
- Generated projects are real runtimes, not scaffold placeholders.
- No source capability is silently lost.
- Every original archived file receives a final disposition.
- `files.txt` becomes the canonical per-file checklist used during implementation.
- The archive can be deleted after parity is proven.

# Constraints
- Keep installer manifest-driven.
- Do not depend on archive files at runtime.
- Preserve original logical architecture even if physical packaging is simplified.
- Use feature packs for optional or heavy capability families.

## Success Criteria
- `PLAN.md` is fully represented by the bead graph.
- `files.txt` is used as the canonical migration checklist.
- All three language lanes reach real runtime parity for the agreed core slice.
- Every archived file has a final disposition before archive deletion.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `PLAN.md` is fully represented by the bead graph.
- `files.txt` is used as the canonical migration checklist.
- All three language lanes reach real runtime parity for the agreed core slice.
- Every archived file has a final disposition before archive deletion.

title: Normalize inventory, governance, and source-of-truth artifacts
description:
Own the planning and inventory foundation for the migration. This includes `PLAN.md`, `files.txt`, canonical source identities, checklist rules, and all repo-level migration documentation needed so agents never lose track of a file or capability.

## Success Criteria
- `PLAN.md` remains the authoritative migration plan.
- `files.txt` is preserved as the canonical file checklist.
- Repo docs no longer have stale archive naming assumptions.
- Future agents can understand exact source coverage without reopening the archive tree manually.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `PLAN.md` remains the authoritative migration plan.
- `files.txt` is preserved as the canonical file checklist.
- Repo docs no longer have stale archive naming assumptions.
- Future agents can understand exact source coverage without reopening the archive tree manually.

title: Convert files.txt into the active per-file port checklist
description:
# Why
The migration cannot safely cover 2,186 archived source files unless the canonical file list is operationalized as an active checklist.

# Scope
- Convert `files.txt` from a passive path dump into an implementation checklist.
- Preserve canonical source identity for every file.
- Define how beads claim and complete file coverage.
- Record how completed beads must mark their covered files.

# Source coverage
This bead covers the entire `files.txt` inventory across Python, TypeScript, and Rust.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `files.txt` uses an explicit checklist format.
- Every file can be marked complete without ambiguity.
- The checklist format supports future bead-to-file mapping and closeout discipline.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `files.txt` uses an explicit checklist format.
- Every file can be marked complete without ambiguity.
- The checklist format supports future bead-to-file mapping and closeout discipline.
close_reason: done

title: Normalize original source identities against current references layout
description:
# Why
The original source identities are still expressed in legacy prefixes like `./typescript/...`, while the archive now lives under `references/...`. The migration needs both identities preserved.

# Scope
- Preserve original source path identity.
- Preserve current archived evidence path identity.
- Ensure planning artifacts can survive eventual archive deletion.
- Remove stale path assumptions from repo-level migration docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All migration docs use a consistent normalization rule.
- Original source identity is not lost.
- Current archived evidence paths are discoverable and auditable.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- All migration docs use a consistent normalization rule.
- Original source identity is not lost.
- Current archived evidence paths are discoverable and auditable.
close_reason: done

title: Create exhaustive command port table and disposition map
description:
# Why
The command surface is too large to port by intuition. Every original command must be assigned a final disposition.

# Scope
- Build `docs/porting/command-port-table.md`.
- Classify every archived command entry as core, feature-pack, deferred, rejected, or reference-only.
- Map each command to its owner module, language coverage, and verification target.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every archived command has one row in the command port table.
- No command is left implicit.
- Table is usable as an implementation queue and as a deletion gate for the archive.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every archived command has one row in the command port table.
- No command is left implicit.
- Table is usable as an implementation queue and as a deletion gate for the archive.
close_reason: done

title: Create exhaustive tool port table and disposition map
description:
# Why
The tool surface is one of the highest-risk areas for silent loss. Every original tool and tool-support entry must be accounted for.

# Scope
- Build `docs/porting/tool-port-table.md`.
- Classify every tool as core, feature-pack, deferred, rejected, or reference-only.
- Capture owner modules, language coverage, and test obligations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every archived tool has one row in the tool port table.
- The final table is implementation-ready and archive-retirement-safe.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every archived tool has one row in the tool port table.
- The final table is implementation-ready and archive-retirement-safe.
close_reason: done

title: Create exhaustive feature-pack family map from the plan and archive
description:
# Why
The boilerplate must stay clean, so optional capability families need a disciplined feature-pack map before implementation expands.

# Scope
- Build `docs/porting/feature-pack-port-table.md`.
- Map each optional family from `PLAN.md` to concrete responsibilities, manifests, patches, tests, and MVP skill expectations.
- Capture cross-language ownership expectations.
- Distinguish add-only feature delivery from generic `.agents` skill delivery and optional native runtime roots.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every optional capability family is represented in the feature-pack port table.
- The table reflects add-only feature semantics rather than reversible package-manager semantics.
- The table is detailed enough that agents can implement packs without reopening the whole plan.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every optional capability family is represented in the feature-pack port table.
- The table reflects add-only feature semantics rather than reversible package-manager semantics.
- The table is detailed enough that agents can implement packs without reopening the whole plan.
close_reason: Contract updated for add-only feature packs and MVP skill delivery

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Implemented add-only feature-pack contract updates in docs/porting/feature-pack-port-table.md and aligned feature-family mapping with .agents skill delivery.

title: Keep PLAN.md, capability matrix, and bead graph mutually consistent
description:
# Why
The migration has three truth surfaces: `PLAN.md`, `shared/docs/capability-matrix.json`, and the bead graph. They must evolve together.

# Scope
- Audit capability state and target bucket claims continuously.
- Prevent drift between docs and implementation.
- Record when a capability is only scaffolded versus behaviorally ported.
- Keep the truth surfaces aligned with the `.agents` + native-hidden-root contract and the one-feature-one-skill MVP.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Capability state is never overstated.
- Plan, matrix, and beads describe the same migration reality.
- Contract shifts trigger explicit audit and reopen work instead of silent divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Capability state is never overstated.
- Plan, matrix, and beads describe the same migration reality.
- Contract shifts trigger explicit audit and reopen work instead of silent divergence.
close_reason: Truth surfaces realigned with current contract and audit evidence

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Aligned PLAN.md, RULES.md, capability matrix, parity audit, and bead graph with the .agents + native-hidden-root contract. Downgraded overclaims and recorded audit evidence.

title: Classify non-runtime archive artifacts and remove noise from the port queue
description:
# Why
The archive contains session logs, screenshots, lockfiles, parity harness assets, plans, and other non-runtime artifacts. They still need a tracked disposition so they do not create confusion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All non-runtime artifacts have an explicit disposition.
- Engineers can distinguish implementation files from evidence-only files quickly.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- All non-runtime artifacts have an explicit disposition.
- Engineers can distinguish implementation files from evidence-only files quickly.
close_reason: done

title: Extract and verify shared core runtime plus installer parity
description:
Own the shared cross-language runtime contract: init, doctor, add-only feature lifecycle, prompt loop, tool loop, permissions, context, session, provider abstraction, and tests. This epic is the gate before any advanced feature-pack work is considered complete.

## Success Criteria
- A fresh generated project in every language has a real core runtime.
- Installer lifecycle tests prove init/add/doctor against the real runtime.
- Core capability states can be promoted based on automated evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- A fresh generated project in every language has a real core runtime.
- Installer lifecycle tests prove init/add/doctor against the real runtime.
- Core capability states can be promoted based on automated evidence.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Brand contract update: generated projects now use top-level <BRAND>.md plus branded compat/config surfaces .<brand>.json and .<brand>/. .agents is no longer the primary runtime root; keep it only as an optional interoperability skill mirror where intentionally shipped.

title: Freeze the generated-project contract across Python, TypeScript, and Rust
description:
# Why
The installer and all language packs need one stable target contract before deep runtime work proceeds.

# Scope
- Finalize required generated-project directories and config surfaces.
- Lock the minimal shared runtime concepts across languages.
- Ensure generated projects stay configurable through both `.agents/*` assets and native hidden runtime roots such as `.<runtime-name>/*`.
- Define `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` as the generic AI development entrypoint for feature-owned best practices.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The contract is documented and testable.
- All three languages target the same semantic concepts with language-native config forms.
- The contract clearly separates contributor authoring trees from generated-project hidden roots.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The contract is documented and testable.
- All three languages target the same semantic concepts with language-native config forms.
- The contract clearly separates contributor authoring trees from generated-project hidden roots.
close_reason: Generated-project contract frozen under new dual-hidden-root model

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Migrated manifest schema, manifests, templates, feature authoring roots, and generated-project contract to .agents plus optional native hidden roots.

title: Implement the shared core session loop contract in all three languages
description:
# Why
A boilerplate that cannot actually run prompt turns is not a real agent runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each language can execute a real session loop.
- Placeholder starter apps are replaced by actual runtime entry behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Each language can execute a real session loop.
- Placeholder starter apps are replaced by actual runtime entry behavior.
close_reason: done

title: Implement provider selection, prompt layering, and context building as real runtime behavior
description:
# Why
The current repo only proves these seams as config structure. The core runtime must actually use them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Configured providers are actually used by the runtime.
- Prompt sections and context roots affect runtime behavior, not only static config.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Configured providers are actually used by the runtime.
- Prompt sections and context roots affect runtime behavior, not only static config.

title: Upgrade permission and sandbox seams into real enforcement logic
description:
# Why
Permission mode and sandboxing are core safety boundaries and cannot remain declarative-only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Permission rules are enforced by runtime behavior.
- Shell and file operations are gated correctly.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Permission rules are enforced by runtime behavior.
- Shell and file operations are gated correctly.

title: Port the mandatory core tool set across all three languages
description:
# Why
File, shell, and web tools define the base usefulness of the boilerplate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The mandatory core tool set works end-to-end in generated projects.
- Tool configs and runtime behavior match.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The mandatory core tool set works end-to-end in generated projects.

title: Implement save, load, resume, export, usage, and cost foundations
description:
# Why
The archive treats sessions as first-class. Generated projects need real persisted state, not only reserved folders.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sessions are persisted and recoverable.
- Usage and cost are tracked by the runtime, not only by directory reservation.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Sessions are persisted and recoverable.
- Usage and cost are tracked by the runtime, not only by directory reservation.
close_reason: done

title: Extend init/doctor/feature lifecycle to validate the real runtime, not just file presence
description:
# Why
The installer is already real, but its checks are still biased toward static scaffolding. Once runtime depth lands, installer validation must keep pace.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` catches runtime drift, not only missing files.
- Feature add remains robust as runtime depth increases, even after user customization of generated code.
- Installer checks validate the declared bootstrap surfaces without relying on reversible removal semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `doctor` catches runtime drift, not only missing files.
- Feature add remains robust as runtime depth increases, even after user customization of generated code.
- Installer checks validate the declared bootstrap surfaces without relying on reversible removal semantics.
close_reason: Init/doctor/feature add lifecycle updated to new contract

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Installer feature lifecycle is now add-only. feature add reads from languages/<id>/features/, materializes .agents assets and skill receipts, and doctor validates the new bootstrap surfaces.

title: Create fixture projects and cross-language parity tests for the core slice
description:
# Why
The current test suite is strong on installer shape, but weak on behavioral parity. Core runtime work must be proven with generated-project fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The core slice is protected by automated integration tests.
- Capabilities can be promoted to verified based on evidence.
- Tests cover the add-only feature lifecycle and the `.agents` plus optional native hidden-root contract.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The core slice is protected by automated integration tests.
- Capabilities can be promoted to verified based on evidence.
- Tests cover the add-only feature lifecycle and the `.agents` plus optional native hidden-root contract.
close_reason: Fixture and governance verification updated to new contract

title: Port Python lane from mirror workspace to real runtime

Replace Python mirror-style and snapshot-style behavior with a true Python runtime kernel that matches the shipped AICD contract and preserves the original system architecture.

## Success Criteria

- Python generated projects have real runtime behavior.
- Every Python archived file is either ported, feature-packed, deferred, or explicitly excluded.

title: Retire Python reference_data from runtime ownership and convert it into docs/spec/test fixtures only
description:
# Why
The Python archive currently leans on `reference_data` as a runtime crutch. That cannot survive into the final boilerplate.

- Local skill discovery remains intact.
- Bundled skill behavior has an explicit port or feature-pack decision.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Brand contract clarification: feature-owned skills should materialize first to .<brand>/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md. .agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md remains only an optional interoperability mirror, not the primary runtime/config surface.

comments.author: OliveGorge
comments.text:
# Closeout evidence

- Local skill discovery remains part of the shipped core slice and is already evidenced in `shared/docs/capability-matrix.json`, template skill roots, and installer skill-materialization tests.
- Bundled and registry-driven skill pipelines now have an explicit non-core shipped story: they remain optional/declared rather than silently absorbed into core (`shared/docs/capability-matrix.json`, `shared/docs/core-feature-policy.md`, `docs/porting/feature-pack-port-table.md`).
- Child coverage bead `aicd-3ix.4.12.1` is now closed after reconciling its 20 claimed `references/typescript/src/skills/**` rows in `files.txt`.
- This satisfies the parent acceptance criteria without overstating bundled pipeline implementation.

# Closeout evidence

- Capability policy confirms the shipped split: `local-skill-discovery` is implemented core behavior, while `bundled-skill-pipelines` remains an optional declared feature-pack family (`shared/docs/capability-matrix.json`).
- Governance/docs match that split: `docs/porting/feature-pack-port-table.md` keeps `bundled-skills` declared, and `shared/docs/core-feature-policy.md` preserves one feature-owned `SKILL.md` per shipped feature.

- Runtime proof remains the already-shipped feature-skill materialization path exercised by installer tests; for this batch I re-ran `cargo test --manifest-path install/Cargo.toml typescript_plugin_feature_packs_add_optional_skills -- --nocapture` to confirm the TypeScript optional skill path remains healthy while reconciling skills coverage.
- Scope note: this bead closes the claimed source-skill rows by final disposition, not by claiming bundled skill pipelines are already shipped.

title: Classify and extract the TypeScript UI, screen, ink, and hook surfaces
description:
Own the large UI-facing and hook-facing surfaces and give each explicit fate: extracted runtime ownership, feature pack, deferred, or excluded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- UI-heavy surfaces are either extracted into clean ownership, feature-packed, or explicitly deferred.
- No UI cluster is ported blindly into core.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- UI-heavy surfaces are either extracted into clean ownership, feature-packed, or explicitly deferred.
- No UI cluster is ported blindly into core.

title: TypeScript coverage: components, screens, ink, and hooks
description:
Cover `src/components/*`, `src/screens/*`, `src/ink/*`, and `src/hooks/*` as one UI-runtime/deferred coverage family.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every UI-facing file has a final disposition.
- Deferred UI files are explicitly tracked rather than forgotten.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every UI-facing file has a final disposition.
- Deferred UI files are explicitly tracked rather than forgotten.

title: Port the TypeScript core tool set into shipped runtime ownership
description:
Port the base tool set under `src/tools/*` that belongs in the mandatory runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Base tool set works end-to-end in the TypeScript runtime.
- Registry composition remains recognizable relative to the original system.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Base tool set works end-to-end in the TypeScript runtime.
- Registry composition remains recognizable relative to the original system.
close_reason: done

## Acceptance Criteria
- Core tool files all map to shipped runtime ownership.
- Base tool rows in `files.txt` can be ticked bead-by-bead.
acceptance_criteria:

- Core tool files all map to shipped runtime ownership.

title: Extract and adapt Rust runtime lane into the language-pack contract
description:
Promote the already-rich Rust runtime reconstruction into the shipped Rust language-pack boundary so generated Rust projects inherit the real runtime instead of placeholder scaffolding.

## Success Criteria
- Rust language pack contains a real runtime kernel.
- Generated Rust projects inherit real command, tool, session, provider, and safety behavior.
- Every archived Rust file receives a final disposition.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Rust language pack contains a real runtime kernel.
- Generated Rust projects inherit real command, tool, session, provider, and safety behavior.
- Every archived Rust file receives a final disposition.

title: Freeze Rust workspace ownership and crate-to-language-pack mapping
description:
Map every archived Rust crate and workspace artifact to its shipped destination or disposition.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rust workspace structure is fully understood and mapped to the language-pack contract.
- No crate or workspace artifact is left without a disposition.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Rust workspace structure is fully understood and mapped to the language-pack contract.
- No crate or workspace artifact is left without a disposition.
close_reason: done

title: Port the Rust CLI crate entrypoint, args, input, render, and init flow
description:
Own the Rust CLI crate entry behavior so generated Rust projects inherit a real runtime shell.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generated Rust projects inherit real CLI entry behavior from the shipped runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Generated Rust projects inherit real CLI entry behavior from the shipped runtime.

title: Classify Rust harnesses, telemetry crate, scripts, and historical artifacts
description:
Own mock service/harness artifacts, telemetry crate, parity scripts, mock scenarios, and other non-runtime Rust evidence files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every Rust non-runtime artifact has a final disposition.
- Useful parity harness pieces survive as test fixtures where needed.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every Rust non-runtime artifact has a final disposition.
- Useful parity harness pieces survive as test fixtures where needed.

title: Rust coverage: workspace root docs, scripts, sessions, plans, and historical artifacts
description:
Cover root Rust workspace docs, parity docs, scripts, session logs, local plans, mock scenarios, and other non-runtime archive artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every covered Rust non-runtime file has a final disposition.
- Archive noise is separated cleanly from shipped runtime ownership.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every covered Rust non-runtime file has a final disposition.
- Archive noise is separated cleanly from shipped runtime ownership.

title: Ship advanced feature-pack families without losing capability breadth
description:
Own all optional capability families that should not bloat the base core runtime: planning, MCP, LSP, Git workflows, onboarding/auth UX, plugins, remote transport, memory, hooks, and prompt-suggestion families.

## Success Criteria
- Optional capability families are packaged cleanly as add-only feature packs.
- Where appropriate, feature packs ship one reusable `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` entrypoint that carries feature best practices forward for later LLM-driven development.
- No heavy optional subsystem leaks into mandatory core accidentally.
- Feature-pack manifests and tests are strong enough to survive archive removal.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Optional capability families are packaged cleanly as add-only feature packs.
- Where appropriate, feature packs ship one reusable `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` entrypoint that carries feature best practices forward for later LLM-driven development.
- No heavy optional subsystem leaks into mandatory core accidentally.
- Feature-pack manifests and tests are strong enough to survive archive removal.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: Brand contract clarification: optional feature packs should target branded runtime roots first (. <brand>/agents, .<brand>/skills, .<brand>/commands, .<brand>-plugin/plugin.json as applicable). Do not treat .agents as the primary branded runtime root.

title: Design and ship the advanced planning feature-pack family
description:
Own plan-mode, deep-plan, todo/workflow-control, and related non-core planning behaviors as a coherent add-only pack family.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Planning pack boundaries are explicit.
- Core runtime stays lean while advanced planning remains available.
- The planning feature family can ship one reusable skill entrypoint under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` when that improves follow-on development.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Planning pack boundaries are explicit.
- Core runtime stays lean while advanced planning remains available.
- The planning feature family can ship one reusable skill entrypoint under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` when that improves follow-on development.

title: Design and ship the multi-agent workflow feature-pack family
description:
Own task/team/coordinator/swarm-style behavior as a dedicated optional pack family across languages.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-agent/task/team features are packaged and tested as optional runtime layers.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Multi-agent/task/team features are packaged and tested as optional runtime layers.

title: Design and ship the MCP integration feature-pack family
description:
Own MCP resource access, tool invocation, auth, lifecycle, and transport behavior as one explicit capability family.

## Acceptance Criteria
- MCP pack boundaries are clear for an add-only feature lifecycle.
- Language-specific MCP implementations map back to one cross-language contract.
- If the MCP feature is shipped, it can also ship one reusable MCP workflow skill under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- MCP pack boundaries are clear for an add-only feature lifecycle.
- Language-specific MCP implementations map back to one cross-language contract.
- If the MCP feature is shipped, it can also ship one reusable MCP workflow skill under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.

title: Design and ship the LSP tooling feature-pack family
description:
Own diagnostics, symbols, references, hover, and related language-server behavior as an optional pack.

## Acceptance Criteria
- LSP support is coherent for an add-only feature lifecycle.
- If the LSP feature is shipped, it can also ship one reusable LSP workflow skill under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- LSP support is coherent for an add-only feature lifecycle.
- If the LSP feature is shipped, it can also ship one reusable LSP workflow skill under `.agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.

title: Design and ship the Git and GitHub workflow feature-pack family
description:
Own branch, diff, commit, PR, issue, and review automation families as optional workflow packs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Git/GitHub behaviors have a final packaged architecture.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Git/GitHub behaviors have a final packaged architecture.

title: Design and ship the onboarding and auth UX feature-pack family
description:
Own login, logout, OAuth UX, onboarding, install-app flows, and similar guided setup surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Auth/onboarding behavior is available without bloating the base runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Auth/onboarding behavior is available without bloating the base runtime.

title: Design and ship the plugin runtime and marketplace feature-pack family
description:
Own plugin manifests, source kinds, installation lifecycle, marketplace behavior, plugin tools/hooks/commands, and related validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Plugin behavior has a disciplined packaged architecture.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Plugin behavior has a disciplined packaged architecture.

title: Design and ship the remote transport, bridge, proxy, and direct-connect feature-pack family
description:
Own structured transport, bridge runtime, upstream proxy, relay, and direct-connect/server behaviors as optional advanced runtime families.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remote/bridge capabilities are explicitly packaged and testable.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Remote/bridge capabilities are explicitly packaged and testable.

title: Design and ship memory, team-memory, compaction, hook-runtime, and prompt-suggestion families
description:
Own memory augmentation, team memory, compaction beyond core, hooks, prompt suggestions, and magic-doc-style helper families.

## Acceptance Criteria
- These capabilities are packaged explicitly and do not leak into core accidentally.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- These capabilities are packaged explicitly and do not leak into core accidentally.

title: Prove completeness, maintain checklist discipline, and retire the archive
description:
Own the final completeness gates: per-file checklists, port tables, cross-reference audits, archive independence, and release readiness. This epic closes the project only when the archive can be deleted safely.

## Success Criteria
- `files.txt` is actively used to track port completion.
- Command, tool, and feature-pack port tables exist and are complete.
- Archive-independent tests and docs are in place.
- The project can retire the archive without losing migration truth.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `files.txt` is actively used to track port completion.
- Command, tool, and feature-pack port tables exist and are complete.
- Archive-independent tests and docs are in place.
- The project can retire the archive without losing migration truth.

title: Define and enforce the bead-close protocol for ticking files.txt
description:
# Why
The checklist is only useful if completion discipline is explicit. This bead defines how every implementation bead must update `files.txt` as it lands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- There is a clear rule for claiming and ticking file rows.
- Future agents can close beads without ambiguity about checklist maintenance.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- There is a clear rule for claiming and ticking file rows.
- Future agents can close beads without ambiguity about checklist maintenance.
close_reason: Completed: define the canonical files.txt bead-close protocol and checklist maintenance rules

title: Annotate every implementation bead with explicit source-file coverage clusters
description:
Ensure every implementation bead identifies the archived file regions it owns so file-ticking can happen deterministically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every implementation bead has explicit source coverage notes.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Every implementation bead has explicit source coverage notes.

title: Checklist coverage: map every bead to files.txt regions before implementation begins
description:
Ensure every implementation bead declares which `files.txt` rows it owns before coding begins so checklist ticking is deterministic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation beads have explicit `files.txt` coverage regions.
- No file is left without a bead owner.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Implementation beads have explicit `files.txt` coverage regions.
- No file is left without a bead owner.

title: Create archive-independence docs, tests, and removal gates
description:
# Scope
- Ensure no shipped runtime depends on archive paths.
- Ensure docs/specs/tests preserve migration truth.
- Define the final archive deletion gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Archive removal can happen safely once all implementation and checklist gates pass.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Archive removal can happen safely once all implementation and checklist gates pass.
close_reason: done

title: Coverage audit: detect files.txt rows that still lack a bead owner
description:
Run periodic audits to detect uncovered `files.txt` rows and open new beads before implementation starts drifting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The team can prove that every `files.txt` row belongs to at least one bead.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The team can prove that every `files.txt` row belongs to at least one bead.

title: Polish, lint, deduplicate, and dependency-check the bead graph before implementation swarms start
description:
Use `br lint`, dependency inspection, and manual review to ensure the bead graph is coherent, non-duplicative, and ready for long-running execution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bead graph has no obvious duplication or dependency breakage.
- Ready work can be dispatched confidently.
acceptance_criteria:

- Bead graph has no obvious duplication or dependency breakage.

title: Polish pass: keep bead graph granular enough for 2186-file coverage
description:
Guard against under-granular planning by revisiting bead size whenever a bead cannot reasonably own and verify its assigned archive region.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bead graph stays fine-grained enough to control 2,186 archived files safely.
acceptance_criteria:

- The bead graph stays fine-grained enough to control 2,186 archived files safely.
